synthesis_text: The most common symptoms of COVID-19 include cough, fever, and loss of smell and taste. Other symptoms may encompass fatigue, breathing difficulties, muscle pain, sore throat, and diarrhea. Loss of taste and smell can occur in up to 88% of symptomatic cases without prior ear, nose, or throat disorders. Symptoms can vary based on the variant and may change over time, ranging from mild to severe, with severe cases potentially leading to shortness of breath and hospitalization [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00011][doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00012].

The most common symptoms of COVID-19 include fever, cough, fatigue, and loss of taste or smell. Other symptoms may encompass shortness of breath, body aches, headaches, sore throat, and digestive issues like diarrhea. Symptoms typically appear 2 to 14 days after exposure, and some individuals may experience worsening symptoms around 5 to 7 days after onset. Severe symptoms requiring emergency help include trouble breathing, persistent chest pain, and new confusion [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00002][doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00006][doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00005].

The most common symptoms of COVID-19 include fever, cough, and tiredness. Other symptoms can encompass shortness of breath, chest pain, muscle aches, headache, and loss of taste or smell. Additional symptoms may include confusion, sore throat, congestion, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, and skin rashes. Infants may experience difficulty feeding, while children generally exhibit similar symptoms but with less severity compared to adults [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00001].
